text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by processing vast amounts of written material, such as books, articles, and websites, which is called training data. During training, the model learns patterns in language, such as grammar and context, by predicting the next word in a sentence. To use an LLM, people often apply a technique called prompt engineering – carefully crafting input questions or instructions to get the best results. These models have impressive capabilities, like writing essays, answering questions, and even creating code. However, they have limits. For example, they can make up false information, called “hallucinations,” and they do not truly understand facts or emotions, as they just rely on learned patterns.
